activation_semantics.next_lawful_action: use this activation view to execute only the bounded packet owned by the selected lane; completion still requires receipt-backed evidence and does not transfer root-session write authority; if execution evidence is still missing, continue bounded diagnosis/reroute rather than treating this view as completion

blocker_reason: selected host/backend returned only an activation view without execution evidence

init.mode_selection_rule: select one worker-safe thinking mode per step inside the assigned bounded scope; do not widen into orchestrator/meta reasoning without an explicit packet trigger

next_steps: activation looks ready enough for normal orchestrator and worker initialization

recommended_flow: create or update one bounded design document before code execution when the request asks for research/specification/planning and implementation together
open one feature epic and one spec-pack task in vida taskflow before delegated implementation begins
use vida docflow to initialize, finalize, and validate the design document
close the spec-pack task and shape the execution packet from the bounded file set and proof targets recorded in the design document

delegate normal write-producing work through the default carrier tier ladder and let runtime pick the cheapest capable tier with a healthy local score instead of collapsing directly into root-session coding

reporting_contract.mode_selection_note: the reporting label must reflect the selected worker-safe per-step thinking mode without exposing hidden reasoning
reporting_contract.scope: worker-facing bounded status and completion reports

vendor_basis.anthropic: structured prompt templates, explicit sections, and evaluation-backed refinement loops
vendor_basis.microsoft: record one bounded decision/design artifact per change and route on explicit cost-quality tradeoffs instead of ad hoc escalation
vendor_basis.openai: structured role configs and reasoning-effort tuning in Codex project config; prefer explicit task/runtime settings over implicit chat heuristics

minimal.description: Minimal development flow with design-gate specification, optional execution preparation, and bounded implementation.

party_chat_council_large.description: Large Party Chat board for cross-domain systemic decisions.

party_chat_council_modern_full.description: Expanded modern Party Chat board with analysis, design, QA, accessibility, release, platform, and governance seats.

party_chat_council_small.description: Small Party Chat board for bounded architecture and conflict decisions.

reviewed.description: Reviewed development flow with design-gate specification, optional execution preparation, implementation, and coach review.

verified.description: Verified development flow with design-gate specification, optional execution preparation, implementation, coach review, and independent verification.

dispatch_aliases.description: Internal VIDA Codex coach alias for packet-local implemented-result versus approved-spec conformance.
dispatch_aliases.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_coach dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: middle.
2. Runtime role: coach.
3. Mission: judge whether the implemented result matches the approved spec, acceptance criteria, and definition of done.

Boundaries:
1. Coach is formative review only.
2. Do not replace independent verification.
3. Do not rewrite the implementation unless the bounded packet explicitly grants that authority.
4. Return either bounded approval-forward or bounded rework with concrete drift evidence.

dispatch_aliases.description: Internal VIDA Codex escalation alias for hard conflicts and cross-scope arbitration when normal closure cannot proceed.
dispatch_aliases.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_escalation dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: architect.
2. Runtime role: solution_architect.
3. Mission: resolve architecture conflicts, handoff incoherence, or cross-scope blockers that normal execution cannot close.

Boundaries:
1. Do not become the normal default lane.
2. Prefer decisions that restore one-owner bounded packet closure.
3. Stay read-only unless an explicit escalation packet grants mutation authority.

dispatch_aliases.description: Internal VIDA Codex execution-preparation alias for architecture preparation and developer handoff shaping before code execution.
dispatch_aliases.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_execution_preparation dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: architect.
2. Runtime role: solution_architect.
3. Mission: produce one bounded architecture-preparation result and lawful developer handoff before implementation begins.

Boundaries:
1. Do not become the default implementation lane.
2. Preserve bounded ownership and packet coherence.
3. Return architecture constraints and handoff artifacts instead of speculative broad redesign.

dispatch_aliases.description: Internal VIDA Codex implementer alias for one bounded write-producing packet.
dispatch_aliases.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_implementer dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: junior.
2. Runtime role: worker.
3. Mission: execute one bounded write-producing packet and return concrete delivery evidence.

Boundaries:
1. Do not self-approve closure.
2. Do not replace coach, verifier, or escalation lanes.
3. Do not widen from one bounded packet into epic or milestone scope.
4. If the packet is missing owned paths, definition of done, verification target, or stop rules, fail closed and return the packet for reshaping.

dispatch_aliases.description: Internal VIDA Codex specification alias for bounded research, specification, and implementation-plan shaping before execution.
dispatch_aliases.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_specification dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: middle.
2. Runtime role: business_analyst.
3. Mission: produce one bounded specification or design-gate result that can lawfully hand off into execution shaping.

Boundaries:
1. Stay inside the approved bounded unit.
2. Do not begin implementation.
3. Return explicit evidence, scope, and proof targets for the next lane.

dispatch_aliases.description: Internal VIDA Codex verifier alias for independent proof, risk review, and closure readiness.
dispatch_aliases.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_verifier dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: senior.
2. Runtime role: verifier.
3. Mission: perform independent proof, bounded verification, and closure-readiness review.

Boundaries:
1. Stay independent from implementer and coach conclusions.
2. Do not collapse into formative coaching.
3. Do not widen from one bounded proof packet into milestone-wide approval.
4. Fail closed when proof, evidence, or acceptance coverage is missing.

tiers.description: Level 1 Codex lane for cheap bounded implementation packets that own one explicit write scope and return concrete delivery evidence without self-approving closure.

Level 2 Codex lane for bounded specification, planning, spec-conformance coach review, and medium implementation packets when the work still fits one packet and does not require senior proof or architecture escalation.
Level 3 Codex lane for independent verification, proof-strength review, and high-confidence closure gates that fail closed on missing evidence.
Level 4 Codex lane for architecture preparation, hard escalation, and cross-scope conflict resolution when the normal implementer-coach-verifier path cannot close lawfully.

vendor_basis.anthropic: structured prompt templates, explicit sections, and evaluation-backed refinement loops
vendor_basis.microsoft: record one bounded decision/design artifact per change and route on explicit cost-quality tradeoffs instead of ad hoc escalation
vendor_basis.openai: structured role configs and reasoning-effort tuning in Codex project config; prefer explicit task/runtime settings over implicit chat heuristics

roles.description: Level 1 Codex lane for cheap bounded implementation packets that own one explicit write scope and return concrete delivery evidence without self-approving closure.

Level 2 Codex lane for bounded specification, planning, spec-conformance coach review, and medium implementation packets when the work still fits one packet and does not require senior proof or architecture escalation.
Level 3 Codex lane for independent verification, proof-strength review, and high-confidence closure gates that fail closed on missing evidence.
Level 4 Codex lane for architecture preparation, hard escalation, and cross-scope conflict resolution when the normal implementer-coach-verifier path cannot close lawfully.

initial_response.operator_message: publish a concise execution plan before mutating docs, dispatching work, or entering implementation

replanning.trigger_rule: replan after each bounded gate or delegated evidence return before the next write-producing step

root_session_write_guard.required_exception_evidence: Run `vida taskflow recovery latest --json` and `vida taskflow consume continue --json` to confirm runtime artifacts expose the canonical root-session pre-write guard.

todo_sequence: capture research, specification scope, and implementation plan in one bounded design document
create one epic and one spec task in vida taskflow before code execution
keep the design artifact canonical through vida docflow init/finalize-edit/check
close the spec task and shape one bounded execution packet from the approved design before delegated development

items.note: open one epic that will own the feature-level tracked flow before documentation or implementation begins
open one spec-pack task under the epic before authoring the design artifact
capture research, specification scope, and implementation plan in one bounded design document
finalize and validate the bounded design artifact canonically
close the spec-pack task only after the design artifact is finalized and validated
shape TaskFlow handoff from spec-pack through work-pool-pack and dev-pack before delegated implementation dispatch

root_session_write_guard.required_exception_evidence: Run `vida taskflow recovery latest --json` and `vida taskflow consume continue --json` to confirm runtime artifacts expose the canonical root-session pre-write guard.

tracked_flow_bootstrap.bootstrap_command: vida taskflow bootstrap-spec 'Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project design document for a possible VIDA memory implementation path based on that research' --json

dev_task.close_command: vida task close feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-dev --reason 'delegated development packet reached proof-ready closure' --json
dev_task.create_command: vida task create feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-dev 'Dev pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels dev-pack --json
dev_task.ensure_command: vida task ensure feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-dev 'Dev pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels dev-pack --json
dev_task.title: Dev pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...

docflow.finalize_command: vida docflow finalize-edit docs/product/spec/mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-design.md 'record bounded feature design'
docflow.init_command: vida docflow init docs/product/spec/mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-design.md product/spec/mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-design product_spec 'initialize bounded feature design'

epic.close_command: vida task close feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --reason 'feature delivery closed after proof and runtime handoff' --json
epic.create_command: vida task create feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design 'Feature epic: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type epic --status open --labels feature-request --labels spec-first --description 'Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project design document for a possible VIDA memory implementation path based on that research' --json
epic.title: Feature epic: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...

tracked_flow_bootstrap.feature_title: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...
handoff_sequence: finalize and validate bounded design document
shape dev packet in TaskFlow before delegated implementation

spec_task.close_command: vida task close feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-spec --reason 'design packet finalized and handed off into tracked work-pool shaping' --json
spec_task.create_command: vida task create feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-spec 'Spec pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels spec-pack --labels documentation --description 'bounded design/spec packet for the feature request' --json
spec_task.ensure_command: vida task ensure feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-spec 'Spec pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels spec-pack --labels documentation --description 'bounded design/spec packet for the feature request' --json
spec_task.title: Spec pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...

work_pool_task.close_command: vida task close feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-work-pool --reason 'work-pool packet closed after delegated execution packet was shaped' --json
work_pool_task.create_command: vida task create feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-work-pool 'Work-pool pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels work-pool-pack --json
work_pool_task.ensure_command: vida task ensure feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-work-pool 'Work-pool pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels work-pool-pack --json
work_pool_task.title: Work-pool pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...

role_selection.request: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project design document for a possible VIDA memory implementation path based on that research

delivery_task_packet.blocking_question: What is the next bounded action required for `specification`?
definition_of_done: `specification` handoff produces a bounded runtime result artifact
dispatch receipt and downstream preview are refreshed consistently
delivery_task_packet.goal: Execute bounded `specification` handoff for the active runtime request
delivery_task_packet.proof_target: runtime dispatch result artifact plus updated dispatch receipt
delivery_task_packet.request_excerpt: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project design document for a possible VIDA memory implementation path based on that research
stop_rules: stop after writing bounded dispatch result or explicit blocker
do not widen scope beyond the active packet target

packet.downstream_dispatch_note: after spec-pack task materialization, dispatch the business-analyst lane for bounded research/specification/planning before work-pool shaping

initial_response.operator_message: publish a concise execution plan before mutating docs, dispatching work, or entering implementation

replanning.trigger_rule: replan after each bounded gate or delegated evidence return before the next write-producing step

root_session_write_guard.required_exception_evidence: Run `vida taskflow recovery latest --json` and `vida taskflow consume continue --json` to confirm runtime artifacts expose the canonical root-session pre-write guard.

packet.prompt:
Packet run_id=feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-spec
Target=specification
Runtime role=business_analyst
Root session role=orchestrator
Execution mode=delegated_orchestration_cycle
Canonical delegated execution surface=vida agent-init
This packet activation view is not an execution receipt and does not transfer root-session write authority.
If the selected host/backend returns only this activation view without execution evidence, treat that as a bridge blocker, not as delegated work completion.
If a bounded read-only diagnostic path still exists, continue diagnosis to a code-level blocker or next bounded fix before asking the user to choose a route.
Host subagent APIs are backend details only; do not substitute them for the project runtime's delegated lane contract.
Host-local shell/edit capability is not a write-authority receipt.
First substantive response: publish a concise plan before edits or implementation.
Local orchestrator coding is forbidden without an explicit exception path.
Before any local write decision, re-check `vida status --json`, `vida taskflow recovery latest --json`, and `vida taskflow consume continue --json`.
If the user explicitly ordered agent-first or parallel-agent execution, keep that routing sticky and do not silently substitute root-session implementation.
Under continued-development intent, stay in commentary/progress mode; final closure wording is forbidden unless the user explicitly asks to stop.
Do not treat commentary, an intermediate status update, or “I have explained the result” as a lawful pause boundary.
If closure-style wording is emitted by mistake, immediately re-enter commentary mode and bind the next lawful continuation item without waiting.
After any bounded result, green test, successful build, or delegated handoff, immediately bind the next lawful continuation item instead of pausing at a summary.
When recording task notes from shell, prefer `vida task update <task-id> --notes-file <path> --json` over inline shell quoting for complex text.
Finding the patch location, reproducing a runtime defect, or hitting a worker timeout does not authorize root-session fallback; wait, reroute, or record the exception path first.
Agent/thread limits, stale lane handles, or `not_found` carrier failures require saturation recovery first: inspect active lanes, synthesize completed returns, reclaim closeable lanes, and retry lawful `vida agent-init` dispatch before any local fallback is considered.
Replan checkpoints: after_design_gate, after_work_pool_shape, after_dev_packet_shape, after_implementation_evidence, after_review_evidence, after_verification_evidence
Goal: execute only this bounded handoff and produce receipt-backed evidence.
Request: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project design document for a possible VIDA memory implementation path based on that research

reviewed.description: Reviewed development flow with design-gate specification, optional execution preparation, implementation, and coach review.

verified.description: Verified development flow with design-gate specification, optional execution preparation, implementation, coach review, and independent verification.

dispatch_aliases.description: Internal VIDA Codex coach alias for packet-local implemented-result versus approved-spec conformance.
dispatch_aliases.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_coach dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: middle.
2. Runtime role: coach.
3. Mission: judge whether the implemented result matches the approved spec, acceptance criteria, and definition of done.

Boundaries:
1. Coach is formative review only.
2. Do not replace independent verification.
3. Do not rewrite the implementation unless the bounded packet explicitly grants that authority.
4. Return either bounded approval-forward or bounded rework with concrete drift evidence.

dispatch_aliases.description: Internal VIDA Codex escalation alias for hard conflicts and cross-scope arbitration when normal closure cannot proceed.
dispatch_aliases.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_escalation dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: architect.
2. Runtime role: solution_architect.
3. Mission: resolve architecture conflicts, handoff incoherence, or cross-scope blockers that normal execution cannot close.

Boundaries:
1. Do not become the normal default lane.
2. Prefer decisions that restore one-owner bounded packet closure.
3. Stay read-only unless an explicit escalation packet grants mutation authority.

dispatch_aliases.description: Internal VIDA Codex execution-preparation alias for architecture preparation and developer handoff shaping before code execution.
dispatch_aliases.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_execution_preparation dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: architect.
2. Runtime role: solution_architect.
3. Mission: produce one bounded architecture-preparation result and lawful developer handoff before implementation begins.

Boundaries:
1. Do not become the default implementation lane.
2. Preserve bounded ownership and packet coherence.
3. Return architecture constraints and handoff artifacts instead of speculative broad redesign.

dispatch_aliases.description: Internal VIDA Codex implementer alias for one bounded write-producing packet.
dispatch_aliases.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_implementer dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: junior.
2. Runtime role: worker.
3. Mission: execute one bounded write-producing packet and return concrete delivery evidence.

Boundaries:
1. Do not self-approve closure.
2. Do not replace coach, verifier, or escalation lanes.
3. Do not widen from one bounded packet into epic or milestone scope.
4. If the packet is missing owned paths, definition of done, verification target, or stop rules, fail closed and return the packet for reshaping.

dispatch_aliases.description: Internal VIDA Codex specification alias for bounded research, specification, and implementation-plan shaping before execution.
dispatch_aliases.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_specification dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: middle.
2. Runtime role: business_analyst.
3. Mission: produce one bounded specification or design-gate result that can lawfully hand off into execution shaping.

Boundaries:
1. Stay inside the approved bounded unit.
2. Do not begin implementation.
3. Return explicit evidence, scope, and proof targets for the next lane.

dispatch_aliases.description: Internal VIDA Codex verifier alias for independent proof, risk review, and closure readiness.
dispatch_aliases.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_verifier dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: senior.
2. Runtime role: verifier.
3. Mission: perform independent proof, bounded verification, and closure-readiness review.

Boundaries:
1. Stay independent from implementer and coach conclusions.
2. Do not collapse into formative coaching.
3. Do not widen from one bounded proof packet into milestone-wide approval.
4. Fail closed when proof, evidence, or acceptance coverage is missing.

tiers.description: Level 1 Codex lane for cheap bounded implementation packets that own one explicit write scope and return concrete delivery evidence without self-approving closure.

Level 2 Codex lane for bounded specification, planning, spec-conformance coach review, and medium implementation packets when the work still fits one packet and does not require senior proof or architecture escalation.
Level 3 Codex lane for independent verification, proof-strength review, and high-confidence closure gates that fail closed on missing evidence.
Level 4 Codex lane for architecture preparation, hard escalation, and cross-scope conflict resolution when the normal implementer-coach-verifier path cannot close lawfully.

vendor_basis.anthropic: structured prompt templates, explicit sections, and evaluation-backed refinement loops
vendor_basis.microsoft: record one bounded decision/design artifact per change and route on explicit cost-quality tradeoffs instead of ad hoc escalation
vendor_basis.openai: structured role configs and reasoning-effort tuning in Codex project config; prefer explicit task/runtime settings over implicit chat heuristics

roles.description: Level 1 Codex lane for cheap bounded implementation packets that own one explicit write scope and return concrete delivery evidence without self-approving closure.

Level 2 Codex lane for bounded specification, planning, spec-conformance coach review, and medium implementation packets when the work still fits one packet and does not require senior proof or architecture escalation.
Level 3 Codex lane for independent verification, proof-strength review, and high-confidence closure gates that fail closed on missing evidence.
Level 4 Codex lane for architecture preparation, hard escalation, and cross-scope conflict resolution when the normal implementer-coach-verifier path cannot close lawfully.

party_chat_council_large.description: Large Party Chat board for cross-domain systemic decisions.

party_chat_council_modern_full.description: Expanded modern Party Chat board with analysis, design, QA, accessibility, release, platform, and governance seats.

party_chat_council_small.description: Small Party Chat board for bounded architecture and conflict decisions.

party_chat_accessibility_ux.description: Accessibility and UX conformance seat for usability risks, inclusive interaction review, and interface-quality rework guidance.

party_chat_architect.description: Architecture-first council seat for system shape, structure, boundary design, and implementation-constraint framing before execution changes are proposed.

party_chat_data_contracts.description: Specialty seat for schemas, contracts, compatibility surfaces, and data-boundary reasoning.

party_chat_delivery_cost.description: Delivery and cost trade-off seat for feasibility, rollout shape, sequencing, and bounded budget consequences.

party_chat_dx_tooling.description: Specialty seat for tooling, developer experience, operator ergonomics, and workflow friction reduction.

party_chat_facilitator.description: Facilitates Party Chat scope framing, board assembly, DEI checks, and synthesis boundaries so the council stays inside one explicit decision surface.

party_chat_pm_process.description: Specialty seat for delivery process, sequencing, escalation workflow, and route-level governance hygiene.

party_chat_product_designer.description: Product-design seat for user flows, interaction shape, information hierarchy, and experience constraints that should guide implementation.

party_chat_product_scope.description: Product scope seat for outcome framing, prioritization, and user-facing consequences of the proposed change.

party_chat_qa_tester.description: QA and testing seat for scenario coverage, failure modes, regression risk, and executable test expectations.

party_chat_quality_verification.description: Verification seat for critique, acceptance-criteria conformance, failure signals, and confidence in closure readiness.

party_chat_release_manager.description: Release-management seat for rollout gating, release slicing, dependency timing, and go/no-go coordination signals.

party_chat_runtime_systems.description: Runtime and orchestration seat for execution substrate, operator-facing runtime behavior, and state-transition consequences.

party_chat_security_safety.description: Specialty seat for security and safety concerns, abuse cases, and fail-closed risk posture in Party Chat escalation boards.

party_chat_sre_observability.description: Specialty seat for reliability, observability, and operational-readiness conformance before work is considered ready to proceed.

party_chat_system_analyst.description: System-analysis seat for requirements decomposition, business-rule tracing, interface expectations, and ambiguity removal before execution.

initial_response.operator_message: publish a concise execution plan before mutating docs, dispatching work, or entering implementation

replanning.trigger_rule: replan after each bounded gate or delegated evidence return before the next write-producing step

root_session_write_guard.required_exception_evidence: Run `vida taskflow recovery latest --json` and `vida taskflow consume continue --json` to confirm runtime artifacts expose the canonical root-session pre-write guard.

todo_sequence: capture research, specification scope, and implementation plan in one bounded design document
create one epic and one spec task in vida taskflow before code execution
keep the design artifact canonical through vida docflow init/finalize-edit/check
close the spec task and shape one bounded execution packet from the approved design before delegated development

items.note: open one epic that will own the feature-level tracked flow before documentation or implementation begins
open one spec-pack task under the epic before authoring the design artifact
capture research, specification scope, and implementation plan in one bounded design document
finalize and validate the bounded design artifact canonically
close the spec-pack task only after the design artifact is finalized and validated
shape TaskFlow handoff from spec-pack through work-pool-pack and dev-pack before delegated implementation dispatch

root_session_write_guard.required_exception_evidence: Run `vida taskflow recovery latest --json` and `vida taskflow consume continue --json` to confirm runtime artifacts expose the canonical root-session pre-write guard.

tracked_flow_bootstrap.bootstrap_command: vida taskflow bootstrap-spec 'Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project design document for a possible VIDA memory implementation path based on that research' --json

dev_task.close_command: vida task close feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-dev --reason 'delegated development packet reached proof-ready closure' --json
dev_task.create_command: vida task create feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-dev 'Dev pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels dev-pack --json
dev_task.ensure_command: vida task ensure feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-dev 'Dev pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels dev-pack --json
dev_task.title: Dev pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...

docflow.finalize_command: vida docflow finalize-edit docs/product/spec/mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-design.md 'record bounded feature design'
docflow.init_command: vida docflow init docs/product/spec/mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-design.md product/spec/mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-design product_spec 'initialize bounded feature design'

epic.close_command: vida task close feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --reason 'feature delivery closed after proof and runtime handoff' --json
epic.create_command: vida task create feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design 'Feature epic: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type epic --status open --labels feature-request --labels spec-first --description 'Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project design document for a possible VIDA memory implementation path based on that research' --json
epic.title: Feature epic: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...

tracked_flow_bootstrap.feature_title: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...
handoff_sequence: finalize and validate bounded design document
shape dev packet in TaskFlow before delegated implementation

spec_task.close_command: vida task close feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-spec --reason 'design packet finalized and handed off into tracked work-pool shaping' --json
spec_task.create_command: vida task create feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-spec 'Spec pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels spec-pack --labels documentation --description 'bounded design/spec packet for the feature request' --json
spec_task.ensure_command: vida task ensure feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-spec 'Spec pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels spec-pack --labels documentation --description 'bounded design/spec packet for the feature request' --json
spec_task.title: Spec pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...

work_pool_task.close_command: vida task close feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-work-pool --reason 'work-pool packet closed after delegated execution packet was shaped' --json
work_pool_task.create_command: vida task create feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-work-pool 'Work-pool pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels work-pool-pack --json
work_pool_task.ensure_command: vida task ensure feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-work-pool 'Work-pool pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels work-pool-pack --json
work_pool_task.title: Work-pool pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...

party_chat_data_contracts.description: Specialty seat for schemas, contracts, compatibility surfaces, and data-boundary reasoning.

party_chat_delivery_cost.description: Delivery and cost trade-off seat for feasibility, rollout shape, sequencing, and bounded budget consequences.

party_chat_facilitator.description: Facilitates Party Chat scope framing, board assembly, DEI checks, and synthesis boundaries so the council stays inside one explicit decision surface.

party_chat_pm_process.description: Specialty seat for delivery process, sequencing, escalation workflow, and route-level governance hygiene.

party_chat_product_designer.description: Product-design seat for user flows, interaction shape, information hierarchy, and experience constraints that should guide implementation.

party_chat_qa_tester.description: QA and testing seat for scenario coverage, failure modes, regression risk, and executable test expectations.

party_chat_quality_verification.description: Verification seat for critique, acceptance-criteria conformance, failure signals, and confidence in closure readiness.

party_chat_release_manager.description: Release-management seat for rollout gating, release slicing, dependency timing, and go/no-go coordination signals.

party_chat_runtime_systems.description: Runtime and orchestration seat for execution substrate, operator-facing runtime behavior, and state-transition consequences.

party_chat_security_safety.description: Specialty seat for security and safety concerns, abuse cases, and fail-closed risk posture in Party Chat escalation boards.

party_chat_sre_observability.description: Specialty seat for reliability, observability, and operational-readiness conformance before work is considered ready to proceed.

party_chat_system_analyst.description: System-analysis seat for requirements decomposition, business-rule tracing, interface expectations, and ambiguity removal before execution.

coach_activation.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_coach dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: middle.
2. Runtime role: coach.
3. Mission: judge whether the implemented result matches the approved spec, acceptance criteria, and definition of done.

Boundaries:
1. Coach is formative review only.
2. Do not replace independent verification.
3. Do not rewrite the implementation unless the bounded packet explicitly grants that authority.
4. Return either bounded approval-forward or bounded rework with concrete drift evidence.
coach_activation.dispatch_alias_description: Internal VIDA Codex coach alias for packet-local implemented-result versus approved-spec conformance.

implementer_activation.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_implementer dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: junior.
2. Runtime role: worker.
3. Mission: execute one bounded write-producing packet and return concrete delivery evidence.

Boundaries:
1. Do not self-approve closure.
2. Do not replace coach, verifier, or escalation lanes.
3. Do not widen from one bounded packet into epic or milestone scope.
4. If the packet is missing owned paths, definition of done, verification target, or stop rules, fail closed and return the packet for reshaping.
implementer_activation.dispatch_alias_description: Internal VIDA Codex implementer alias for one bounded write-producing packet.

activation.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_specification dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: middle.
2. Runtime role: business_analyst.
3. Mission: produce one bounded specification or design-gate result that can lawfully hand off into execution shaping.

Boundaries:
1. Stay inside the approved bounded unit.
2. Do not begin implementation.
3. Return explicit evidence, scope, and proof targets for the next lane.
activation.dispatch_alias_description: Internal VIDA Codex specification alias for bounded research, specification, and implementation-plan shaping before execution.

activation.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_verifier dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: senior.
2. Runtime role: verifier.
3. Mission: perform independent proof, bounded verification, and closure-readiness review.

Boundaries:
1. Stay independent from implementer and coach conclusions.
2. Do not collapse into formative coaching.
3. Do not widen from one bounded proof packet into milestone-wide approval.
4. Fail closed when proof, evidence, or acceptance coverage is missing.
activation.dispatch_alias_description: Internal VIDA Codex verifier alias for independent proof, risk review, and closure readiness.

initial_response.operator_message: publish a concise execution plan before mutating docs, dispatching work, or entering implementation

replanning.trigger_rule: replan after each bounded gate or delegated evidence return before the next write-producing step

root_session_write_guard.required_exception_evidence: Run `vida taskflow recovery latest --json` and `vida taskflow consume continue --json` to confirm runtime artifacts expose the canonical root-session pre-write guard.

todo_sequence: capture research, specification scope, and implementation plan in one bounded design document
create one epic and one spec task in vida taskflow before code execution
keep the design artifact canonical through vida docflow init/finalize-edit/check
close the spec task and shape one bounded execution packet from the approved design before delegated development

items.note: open one epic that will own the feature-level tracked flow before documentation or implementation begins
open one spec-pack task under the epic before authoring the design artifact
capture research, specification scope, and implementation plan in one bounded design document
finalize and validate the bounded design artifact canonically
close the spec-pack task only after the design artifact is finalized and validated
shape TaskFlow handoff from spec-pack through work-pool-pack and dev-pack before delegated implementation dispatch

root_session_write_guard.required_exception_evidence: Run `vida taskflow recovery latest --json` and `vida taskflow consume continue --json` to confirm runtime artifacts expose the canonical root-session pre-write guard.

tracked_flow_bootstrap.bootstrap_command: vida taskflow bootstrap-spec 'Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project design document for a possible VIDA memory implementation path based on that research' --json

dev_task.close_command: vida task close feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-dev --reason 'delegated development packet reached proof-ready closure' --json
dev_task.create_command: vida task create feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-dev 'Dev pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels dev-pack --json
dev_task.ensure_command: vida task ensure feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-dev 'Dev pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels dev-pack --json
dev_task.title: Dev pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...

docflow.finalize_command: vida docflow finalize-edit docs/product/spec/mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-design.md 'record bounded feature design'
docflow.init_command: vida docflow init docs/product/spec/mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-design.md product/spec/mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-design product_spec 'initialize bounded feature design'

epic.close_command: vida task close feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --reason 'feature delivery closed after proof and runtime handoff' --json
epic.create_command: vida task create feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design 'Feature epic: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type epic --status open --labels feature-request --labels spec-first --description 'Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project design document for a possible VIDA memory implementation path based on that research' --json
epic.title: Feature epic: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...

tracked_flow_bootstrap.feature_title: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...
handoff_sequence: finalize and validate bounded design document
shape dev packet in TaskFlow before delegated implementation

spec_task.close_command: vida task close feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-spec --reason 'design packet finalized and handed off into tracked work-pool shaping' --json
spec_task.create_command: vida task create feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-spec 'Spec pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels spec-pack --labels documentation --description 'bounded design/spec packet for the feature request' --json
spec_task.ensure_command: vida task ensure feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-spec 'Spec pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels spec-pack --labels documentation --description 'bounded design/spec packet for the feature request' --json
spec_task.title: Spec pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...

work_pool_task.close_command: vida task close feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-work-pool --reason 'work-pool packet closed after delegated execution packet was shaped' --json
work_pool_task.create_command: vida task create feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-work-pool 'Work-pool pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels work-pool-pack --json
work_pool_task.ensure_command: vida task ensure feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design-work-pool 'Work-pool pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...' --type task --status open --parent-id feature-mempalace-against-vida-specs-project-design --labels work-pool-pack --json
work_pool_task.title: Work-pool pack: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project ...

role_selection.request: Research MemPalace mechanics against VIDA specs and create a project design document for a possible VIDA memory implementation path based on that research
